title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Amazon Kinesis sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Amazon Kinesis as a target-capable streaming service. nats-sinks does not yet have an AWS streaming sink for durable or near-real-time handoff outside NATS.
proposal: Evaluate and later design an Amazon Kinesis sink using the AWS SDK for Python with explicit partition-key mapping, deterministic idempotency metadata, bounded batching, retry controls, payload encryption compatibility, and safe error classification. The feature must document where Kinesis acceptance fits relative to commit-then-acknowledge because Kinesis is a stream handoff target, not a database transaction.
users: Operators integrating NATS JetStream event custody with AWS streaming analytics, serverless processing, or cross-cloud handoff.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success may occur only after Kinesis returns successful `PutRecords` or equivalent acceptance for every required record. Partial failures must prevent ACK for failed messages or route them through explicit retry/DLQ behavior.
security: AWS credentials must use IAM roles, web identity, or secure runtime configuration. Stream names, partition keys, payloads, and metadata must be redacted from logs unless explicitly enabled for controlled diagnostics.
acceptance: Research identifies Kinesis API limits, batch sizes, partition-key rules, and retry behavior.
Design handles partial batch failures without silent message loss.
Configuration validates stream names, region, partition strategy, timeouts, and retry limits.
Unit tests use AWS fakes and make no network calls.
Documentation explains cost, ordering, duplicate delivery, and least-privilege IAM.
tests: Add unit tests for partition key selection, PutRecords batching, partial-failure handling, error translation, and ACK ordering. Live tests must be explicitly gated and sanitized.
documentation: Add docs/amazon-kinesis-sink.md if implemented and update sink framework, configuration, security, idempotency, operations, roadmap, and changelog.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
